title: Update Makefile target to emulate readthedocs automation
body:
Major issue is readthedocs has fixed paths

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@lsm5 Does any of the packaging tools use the Makefile docs target?
so far, only the LICENSE and README.md files are installed by the fedora package as well as the podman-next copr package. So, nothing uses the Makefile docs target yet.
Should the package be building and installing the docs too? I can set that up once this is merged.
@jwhonce are you still working on this?
@umohnani8 I think you can safely take it from him.
@umohnani8 I thought @baude had taken this over. Sorry if I dropped the ball here.
I can take this over and get it done. @jwhonce can you just tell me what exactly we are trying to do here? Is this PR good as is or are some additional changes needed?

@umohnani8 The goal was to have the Makefile target `docs` behaviour be the same as the readthedocs automation concerning where the formatted html landed etc. If my memory is correct it wasn't merged for fear of breaking packaging. Reading @lsm5 's comment it would appear to be safe to merge.
@lsm5 is this safe to merge now? Could we break packaging with this change?

> @lsm5 is this safe to merge now? Could we break packaging with this change?

@umohnani8 I think we can merge this. We can deal with packaging later.
Closing this in favor of https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/282 so we can run tests on latest ci.
